title: Operations Duty Officer (3 days in 3 days out)
description:
Company Overview / Employee Value Proposition

B&H Worldwide, named the world's Best-in-class Aerospace Logistics Supplier and recipient of multiple industry awards, is the market leader in the highly specialist aerospace logistics industry. Over the last 30 years B&H has expanded through a combination of investment and acquisition to become a multi-national specialist aerospace logistics organisation. B&H Worldwide provides comprehensive logistics solutions for the management of aerospace components of any size and any description, anywhere in the world. We are solely dedicated to the aerospace industry. With our broad customer base of leading airlines, spare part stockists, MROs and repair vendors, we are committed to providing specialised solutions for handling critical parts, from aircraft engines and specialist avionics through to components and consumables. Operating across the globe from our strategically located hubs, supported by our highly specialised global AOG centre, B&H is ready to provide industry leading support for all your critical service needs, 24 hours a day, 365 days a year. The B&H brand has become a recognized market leader due to our innovative and IT centric solutions, which are highly tailored to integrate with our customer’s operating models and IT platforms, driving out the cost of process inefficiencies while optimising service performance.

Job Summary

The Duty Officer is responsible for managing import and export shipments end-to-end, ensuring compliance, efficiency, and exceptional customer service. You will coordinate logistics operations, negotiate with suppliers, and collaborate across teams to deliver best-in-class aerospace logistics solutions.

Responsibilities

  • Manage and expedite import/export shipments including AOG and third-party inventory, ensuring timely and compliant delivery aligned with customer requirements
  • Update company systems with detailed shipment comments and actions to maintain transparent workflow and enable seamless handover
  • Respond promptly to calls and emails within Group Service Standards, maintaining communication efficiency and service excellence
  • Investigate and implement cost-effective dispatch options for shipments in accordance with customer instructions
  • Liaise and negotiate with suppliers to secure accurate and timely cost information at booking stage
  • Prepare and manage all necessary documentation and online forms to ensure lawful and efficient import activities
  • Complete booking and execution of Air Waybills (AWBs) and Tradnet permit declarations accurately and efficiently
  • Provide written collection and delivery notes with clear instructions to relevant parties
  • Ensure Customs Entries comply with customer instructions or agreed SOPs, maintaining high standards of accuracy
  • Coordinate with Warehouse and Transport teams to ensure reliable distribution of imports and exports
  • Arrange direct drives and On Board Couriers as requested by customers to meet urgent delivery needs
  • Identify and report any customer service failures, billing discrepancies, or operational errors promptly to management
  • Manage inventory requirements by logging incoming and outgoing shipments stored in the warehouse
  • Maintain and develop customer relationships by collaborating with Customer Solutions and other departments
  • Track shipments and respond to customer enquiries with accurate and timely information
  • Ensure all export and import processes comply with Singapore regulations and internal Quality Management System standards
  • Maintain up-to-date knowledge of import/export laws, tariffs, duties, licenses, and restrictions, sharing insights with colleagues
  • Proactively review operational files to identify opportunities to maximize profitability through effective file management
  • Follow company processes and SOPs to meet KPIs and ensure timely and accurate billing and profit reporting


Personal Attributes

This role needs to be underpinned by excellent customer service skills.  You will have excellent attention to detail and possess a sound knowledge of Import / Export processes.

Key Skills & Experience

·         Proven experience working in Imports / Exports in a customer-facing, fast-paced environment, preferably with aerospace logistics industry.

·         Sound knowledge of Microsoft suite, including Word, Excel and PowerPoint. 

·         The ability to work pro-actively using own initiative.

·         Strong communication skills, both written and verbal.

·         Previous experience dealing with a varied customer base

·         Ability to build strong relationships.

·         Problem solving skills, with the ability to consider several possible options and decide on most appropriate method.

·         Ability to work pro-actively using own initiative.

·         Strong organisational skills with the ability to prioritise as well as focus on the detail

·         Excellent process and planning skills.

·         Strong commercial awareness, understands impact of actions

·         Experience of working with Cargowise and Dangerous Goods Compliant would be an advantage
